title: Workshop Site Manager Takeover
concept: A Site Manager-first Playground redesign for educators running workshops: creation, save state, saved-site management, runtime settings, files, blueprint, database, logs, gallery, and portability all live inside a dark diagnostic manager while the WordPress site is kept as a narrow embedded preview.
focus: Site Manager as the primary product surface
split preview and editor layout with narrow live WordPress preview
dark diagnostics surface with light content cards
concrete source, save, reset, export, import, and Blueprint gallery consequences
flows: Choose a launch source from Vanilla WordPress, WordPress PR, Gutenberg PR or branch, GitHub import, Blueprint URL, or .zip import with distinct inputs and constraints.
Save an unsaved Playground to browser storage or to a local directory, showing copy progress and the resulting saved slug.
Manage saved and unsaved Playgrounds with visible saved identity, rename, delete, and open-list actions.
Use Site Manager tabs for Settings, Files, Blueprint, Database, and Logs while the active WordPress site remains visible in a narrow preview.
Browse the Blueprint gallery with category filters, search, a selected detail card, run, and inspect behavior across a represented 43-entry catalog.
Apply reset/reload decisions, export to GitHub, download a .zip, import a .zip over the current site, download database.sqlite, and open Adminer or phpMyAdmin.
featureCoverage: create from Gutenberg PR or branch
import from GitHub after account connection
browse and filter Blueprint gallery entries
save temporary Playground in browser storage
navigate active WordPress paths with URL input
refresh the active embedded WordPress page
use File browser with create folder/file, upload, browse, and code editing affordances
